title: 'Rust makes coding fun again': Why Linux is moving away from C, according to Greg Kroah-Hartman
excerpt: C won't be disappearing tomorrow, says the stable kernel maintainer, but the future of Linux belongs to Rust.
content_html:
Follow ZDNET: Add us as a preferred source on Google.

ZDNET's key takeaways

  • In the future, some Linux subsystems will only be written in Rust.
  • Rust is much safer than C, but it's no silver bullet for bad logic.
  • The Linux kernel will not be rewritten in Rust. Well, not yet anyway.

At Open Source Summit India 2026 in Mumbai, Linux stable kernel maintainer Greg Kroah‑Hartman said in his keynote that "the [Linux] kernel is moving toward Rust. Git is moving toward Rust. Lots of projects are starting to move toward Rust."

Also: Linus Torvalds puts his foot down, tells anti-AI programmers to 'fork it'

He didn't always feel that way. Kroah-Hartman added, "A number of years ago, when a friend of mine said, 'Ah, you got to try this new language. It's called Rust.' I was like, 'What? No, C is great.' His friend continued, "'No, no, no! It makes programming fun again.' I'm like, 'Nah, programming is fun in C.' He was right. I should have done it then. Rust is actually fun. It makes programming fun. It takes a lot of stuff away from having to worry about the compiler, which can fix a lot of your problems for you, and it makes code a little bit better."

Why Rust makes life so much easier

So, Kroah-Hartman has moved from being a Rust skeptic to one of its strongest champions inside the kernel. He now regards Rust as a permanent part of Linux, not an experiment. His case is straightforward: Rust's ownership and type system can eliminate most of the "stupid little tiny things" that dominate kernel Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs), while making life easier for overworked maintainers. "Rust," in short, "makes my life so much easier."

Kroah-Hartman should know. He oversees the Linux kernel CVE process, and he has a unique vantage point as the stable‑kernel maintainer. In India, he said Linux sees "about 13 CVEs a day" and has been running at "almost nine changes an hour" for a decade or more. Most of those vulnerabilities, he argued, are not exotic attacks but simple C mistakes -- unchecked pointers, forgotten unlocks, and sloppy cleanup paths: "This is what we're fixing 13 times a day. Small, trivial, little bugs like this all the time."

Also: 'I'm not a programmer' anymore: Linus Torvalds on the only two tools he uses now

How can Rust help with this issue? Easy. Kroah-Hartman continued: "Here is my statement, totally unscientific. I've seen every CVE the kernel has done in the past 25 years. I think 80% would be gone, just because they would be caught by Rust." The remaining 20% are the logic bugs he'd prefer to focus on: "Rust will still crash just fine. You can do really foolish things in Rust, but all the tiny, stupid, trivial things Rust will handle for us." 

Kroah‑Hartman consistently framed language choices in terms of reviewer workload rather than developer convenience. Linux has "over 5,000 developers" but "about 150 core maintainers that review the majority of the code," a skew that drives his priorities. "We optimize for reviewers. We don't optimize for developers because we have a lot of developers," he said, suggesting Rust's ability to enforce locking and lifetime rules at build time means reviewers can spend their limited bandwidth on logic rather than bookkeeping: "If it builds as a reviewer, I know it's OK. I can look at the logic."

Also: Open-source security is a mess - IBM and Red Hat bet $5 billion and 20,000 engineers can fix it

The Rust effort has already reshaped C in the kernel. To make Rust bindings safe, maintainers revisited long‑standing C APIs, adding guard and scoped‑allocation mechanisms that mirror Rust's automatic cleanup. "We have the guards, the scoped reference counting, to C because Rust showed us we could," he said. 

"We added a lot more memory safety stuff to the kernel and other APIs because we realized Rust showed us we could do that, and we made the C code more reliable and better. So if Rust were to disappear tomorrow, the kernel's better off for it."

The default for new work in key subsystems

This shift has been coming for some time. As Kroah-Hartman wrote back in 2025 on the Linux Kernel Mailing List (LKML), "Rust also gives us the ability to define our in-kernel APIs in ways that make them almost impossible to get wrong when using them. We have way too many difficult/tricky APIs that require way too much maintainer review to 'ensure that you got this right,' that is a combination of both how our APIs have evolved over the years (how many different ways can you use a 'struct cdev' in a safe way?) and how C doesn't allow us to express APIs in a way that makes them easier/safer to use. Forcing maintainers of these APIs to rethink them is a GOOD thing, as it is causing us to clean them up for EVERYONE, C users included already, making Linux better overall."

Also: Red Hat will support your RHEL forever now - for a price

Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems. "New drivers for some subsystems are only going to be accepted in Rust. Look at the graphics subsystem, the most complex drivers out there that it is today; those guys are going to be in Rust only," he said. Binder, the Android IPC mechanism at the heart of billions of devices, now has parallel C and Rust implementations in the kernel. The C version "will go away soon," leaving the Rust version "as the bedrock of all Android devices going forward."

Formally, the kernel leadership has already blessed Rust as a first‑class language. "This experiment is now no longer an experiment," Kroah‑Hartman said. "The kernel community maintainers came together last year … and we said the Rust experiment is over. It's real. We're going to accept this thing going forward."

Also: IBM and Red Hat launch Lightwell to defend open-source code from AI attacks

Now, that shift doesn't mean the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust. As he said at the Rust Week conference earlier this year in Utrecht, Netherlands, "We don't want rewrites, so unless you're the maintainer and owner of that file, just do it for new stuff. Leave existing C code alone, and let's evolve forward after that."

That said, having watched Linux grow from the start, I get the sense that the day may come when the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust. It won't be anytime soon, but I can see it happening. It's much safer by design. Were someone to ask me today what language they should learn to become a kernel contributor, I'd tell them to pick up Rust. 

Also: I've tested many portable Linux distros, but PorteuX is the one I keep on my USB drive

Meanwhile, Kroah-Hartman acknowledged that "change is hard" for a community steeped in C, including himself: "I like C. I've been doing C for many, many, many years." Indeed, he's been at it for "Longer than many people here have even been alive. It's hard." But he returned to the same theme that first sold him on Rust: "Rust makes coding fun again. I found it makes it so that you don't have to worry about where my references are, where I grab my locks properly, did I do this stuff, did I do this? I can focus on logic, and that's the beauty of it."

Kroah-Hartman continued, "Rust can be more fun for maintainers and more secure Linux for users, and that's the most important thing overall." He ended with that familiar Linux punchline: "And of course, world domination is proceeding as planned."

sentences.zh: Rust 的努力已经在内核中重塑了 C。

sentences.en: To make Rust bindings safe, maintainers revisited long‑standing C APIs, adding guard and scoped‑allocation mechanisms that mirror Rust's automatic cleanup.
sentences.zh: 为了确保 Rust 绑定的安全，维护人员重新审视了长期存在的 C API，添加了反映 Rust 自动清理功能的防护和作用域分配机制。

sentences.en: "We have the guards, the scoped reference counting, to C because Rust showed us we could," he said.
sentences.zh: “我们有 C 的守卫，范围引用计数，因为 Rust 向我们展示了我们可以做到的，”他说。

blocks.html_en: "We added a lot more memory safety stuff to the kernel and other APIs because we realized Rust showed us we could do that, and we made the C code more reliable and better. So if Rust were to disappear tomorrow, the kernel's better off for it."

sentences.en: "We added a lot more memory safety stuff to the kernel and other APIs because we realized Rust showed us we could do that, and we made the C code more reliable and better.
sentences.zh: “我们向内核和其他 API 添加了更多内存安全内容，因为我们意识到 Rust 向我们展示了我们可以做到这一点，并且我们使 C 代码更可靠、更好。

sentences.en: So if Rust were to disappear tomorrow, the kernel's better off for it."
sentences.zh: 因此，如果 Rust 明天就会消失，那么内核就会变得更好。”

blocks.html_en: The default for new work in key subsystems

sentences.en: The default for new work in key subsystems
sentences.zh: 关键子系统中新工作的默认设置

blocks.html_en: This shift has been coming for some time. As Kroah-Hartman wrote back in 2025 on the Linux Kernel Mailing List (LKML), "Rust also gives us the ability to define our in-kernel APIs in ways that make them almost impossible to get wrong when using them. We have way too many difficult/tricky APIs that require way too much maintainer review to 'ensure that you got this right,' that is a combination of both how our APIs have evolved over the years (how many different ways can you use a 'struct cdev' in a safe way?) and how C doesn't allow us to express APIs in a way that makes them easier/safer to use. Forcing maintainers of these APIs to rethink them is a GOOD thing, as it is causing us to clean them up for EVERYONE, C users included already, making Linux better overall."

sentences.en: This shift has been coming for some time.
sentences.zh: 这种转变已经发生了一段时间。

sentences.en: As Kroah-Hartman wrote back in 2025 on the Linux Kernel Mailing List (LKML), "Rust also gives us the ability to define our in-kernel APIs in ways that make them almost impossible to get wrong when using them.
sentences.zh: 正如 Kroah-Hartman 于 2025 年在 Linux 内核邮件列表 (LKML) 上写道的那样，“Rust 还使我们能够定义内核内 API，使它们在使用时几乎不可能出错。

sentences.en: We have way too many difficult/tricky APIs that require way too much maintainer review to 'ensure that you got this right,' that is a combination of both how our APIs have evolved over the years (how many different ways can you use a 'struct cdev' in a safe way?)
sentences.zh: 我们有太多困难/棘手的 API，需要太多的维护者审查来“确保您做对了”，这是我们的 API 多年来发展的结合（您可以以安全的方式使用“struct cdev”有多少种不同的方式？）

sentences.en: and how C doesn't allow us to express APIs in a way that makes them easier/safer to use.
sentences.zh: 以及 C 不允许我们以更容易/更安全使用的方式表达 API。

sentences.en: Forcing maintainers of these APIs to rethink them is a GOOD thing, as it is causing us to clean them up for EVERYONE, C users included already, making Linux better overall."
sentences.zh: 迫使这些 API 的维护者重新考虑它们是一件好事，因为它促使我​​们为所有人（包括 C 用户）清理它们，从而使 Linux 整体变得更好。”

blocks.html_en: Also: Red Hat will support your RHEL forever now - for a price

sentences.en: Also: Red Hat will support your RHEL forever now - for a price
sentences.zh: 另外：红帽现在将永远支持您的 RHEL - 只需支付一定的费用

blocks.html_en: Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems. "New drivers for some subsystems are only going to be accepted in Rust. Look at the graphics subsystem, the most complex drivers out there that it is today; those guys are going to be in Rust only," he said. Binder, the Android IPC mechanism at the heart of billions of devices, now has parallel C and Rust implementations in the kernel. The C version "will go away soon," leaving the Rust version "as the bedrock of all Android devices going forward."

sentences.en: Moreover, Rust is becoming the default for new work in key subsystems.
sentences.zh: 此外，Rust 正在成为关键子系统中新工作的默认设置。

sentences.en: "New drivers for some subsystems are only going to be accepted in Rust.
sentences.zh: “某些子系统的新驱动程序只会在 Rust 中被接受。

sentences.en: Look at the graphics subsystem, the most complex drivers out there that it is today; those guys are going to be in Rust only," he said.
sentences.zh: 看看图形子系统，它是当今最复杂的驱动程序；这些人只会使用 Rust，”他说。

sentences.en: Binder, the Android IPC mechanism at the heart of billions of devices, now has parallel C and Rust implementations in the kernel.
sentences.zh: Binder 是数十亿设备核心的 Android IPC 机制，现在在内核中具有并行的 C 和 Rust 实现。

sentences.en: The C version "will go away soon," leaving the Rust version "as the bedrock of all Android devices going forward."
sentences.zh: C 版本“很快就会消失”，而 Rust 版本“将成为未来所有 Android 设备的基石”。

blocks.html_en: Formally, the kernel leadership has already blessed Rust as a first‑class language. "This experiment is now no longer an experiment," Kroah‑Hartman said. "The kernel community maintainers came together last year … and we said the Rust experiment is over. It's real. We're going to accept this thing going forward."

sentences.en: Formally, the kernel leadership has already blessed Rust as a first‑class language.
sentences.zh: 从形式上来说，内核领导层已经将 Rust 视为一流语言。

sentences.en: "This experiment is now no longer an experiment," Kroah‑Hartman said.
sentences.zh: “这个实验现在不再是一个实验，”克罗哈特曼说。

sentences.en: "The kernel community maintainers came together last year …
sentences.zh: “内核社区维护者去年聚集在一起......

sentences.en: and we said the Rust experiment is over.
sentences.zh: 我们说 Rust 实验已经结束。

sentences.en: It's real.
sentences.zh: 这是真的。

sentences.en: We're going to accept this thing going forward."
sentences.zh: 今后我们将接受这件事。”

blocks.html_en: Also: IBM and Red Hat launch Lightwell to defend open-source code from AI attacks

sentences.en: Also: IBM and Red Hat launch Lightwell to defend open-source code from AI attacks
sentences.zh: 另外：IBM 和红帽推出 Lightwell 来保护开源代码免受人工智能攻击

blocks.html_en: Now, that shift doesn't mean the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust. As he said at the Rust Week conference earlier this year in Utrecht, Netherlands, "We don't want rewrites, so unless you're the maintainer and owner of that file, just do it for new stuff. Leave existing C code alone, and let's evolve forward after that."

sentences.en: Now, that shift doesn't mean the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust.
sentences.zh: 现在，这种转变并不意味着 Linux 内核将用 Rust 重写。

sentences.en: As he said at the Rust Week conference earlier this year in Utrecht, Netherlands, "We don't want rewrites, so unless you're the maintainer and owner of that file, just do it for new stuff.
sentences.zh: 正如他今年早些时候在荷兰乌得勒支举行的 Rust Week 会议上所说，“我们不希望重写，因此除非您是该文件的维护者和所有者，否则只需为新内容重写即可。

sentences.en: Leave existing C code alone, and let's evolve forward after that."
sentences.zh: 保留现有的 C 代码，然后让我们继续发展。”

blocks.html_en: That said, having watched Linux grow from the start, I get the sense that the day may come when the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust. It won't be anytime soon, but I can see it happening. It's much safer by design. Were someone to ask me today what language they should learn to become a kernel contributor, I'd tell them to pick up Rust.

sentences.en: That said, having watched Linux grow from the start, I get the sense that the day may come when the Linux kernel will be rewritten in Rust.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，在见证了 Linux 从一开始的成长之后，我感觉到 Linux 内核将用 Rust 重写的那一天可能会到来。

sentences.en: It won't be anytime soon, but I can see it happening.
sentences.zh: 这不会很快发生，但我可以看到它发生。

sentences.en: It's much safer by design.
sentences.zh: 从设计上来说它更安全。

sentences.en: Were someone to ask me today what language they should learn to become a kernel contributor, I'd tell them to pick up Rust.
sentences.zh: 如果今天有人问我他们应该学习什么语言才能成为内核贡献者，我会告诉他们选择 Rust。

blocks.html_en: Also: I've tested many portable Linux distros, but PorteuX is the one I keep on my USB drive

sentences.en: Also: I've tested many portable Linux distros, but PorteuX is the one I keep on my USB drive
sentences.zh: 另外：我已经测试了许多便携式 Linux 发行版，但 PorteuX 是我保留在 USB 驱动器上的发行版

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, Kroah-Hartman acknowledged that "change is hard" for a community steeped in C, including himself: "I like C. I've been doing C for many, many, many years." Indeed, he's been at it for "Longer than many people here have even been alive. It's hard." But he returned to the same theme that first sold him on Rust: "Rust makes coding fun again. I found it makes it so that you don't have to worry about where my references are, where I grab my locks properly, did I do this stuff, did I do this? I can focus on logic, and that's the beauty of it."

sentences.en: Meanwhile, Kroah-Hartman acknowledged that "change is hard" for a community steeped in C, including himself: "I like C.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，Kroah-Hartman 承认，对于一个沉浸在 C 语言中的社区（包括他自己）来说，“改变是困难的”：“我喜欢 C。

sentences.en: I've been doing C for many, many, many years."
sentences.zh: 我从事 C 语言已经很多很多很多年了。”

sentences.en: Indeed, he's been at it for "Longer than many people here have even been alive.
sentences.zh: 事实上，他从事这项工作的时间“甚至比这里许多人活着的时间还要长。

sentences.en: It's hard."
sentences.zh: 这很难。”

sentences.en: But he returned to the same theme that first sold him on Rust: "Rust makes coding fun again.
sentences.zh: 但他又回到了最初让他爱上 Rust 的主题：“Rust 再次让编码变得有趣。

sentences.en: I found it makes it so that you don't have to worry about where my references are, where I grab my locks properly, did I do this stuff, did I do this?
sentences.zh: 我发现它让你不必担心我的参考资料在哪里，我在哪里正确地抓住了我的锁，我做了这个东西吗？我做了这个吗？

sentences.en: I can focus on logic, and that's the beauty of it."
sentences.zh: 我可以专注于逻辑，这就是它的美妙之处。”

blocks.html_en: Kroah-Hartman continued, "Rust can be more fun for maintainers and more secure Linux for users, and that's the most important thing overall." He ended with that familiar Linux punchline: "And of course, world domination is proceeding as planned."

sentences.en: Kroah-Hartman continued, "Rust can be more fun for maintainers and more secure Linux for users, and that's the most important thing overall."
sentences.zh: Kroah-Hartman 继续说道，“Rust 对维护人员来说更有趣，对用户来说 Linux 更安全，这是最重要的事情。”

sentences.en: He ended with that familiar Linux punchline: "And of course, world domination is proceeding as planned."
sentences.zh: 他以熟悉的 Linux 妙语结束：“当然，世界统治正在按计划进行。”

content_html_zh:
关注 ZDNET：将我们添加为 Google 上的首选来源。

ZDNET 的主要要点

  • 未来，一些 Linux 子系统将只用 Rust 编写。
  • Rust 比 C 安全得多，但它并不是解决糟糕逻辑的灵丹妙药。
  • Linux 内核不会用 Rust 重写。 好吧，无论如何还没有。

在孟买举行的 2026 年印度开源峰会上，Linux 稳定内核维护者 Greg Kroah-Hartman 在他的主题演讲中表示，“[Linux] 内核正在向 Rust 方向发展。 Git 正在向 Rust 迈进。 许多项目开始转向 Rust。”

另外：Linus Torvalds 坚决反对，告诉反人工智能程序员“分叉它”

他并不总是有这样的感觉。 克罗哈特曼补充道：“几年前，当我的一个朋友说，‘啊，你必须尝试这种新语言。 它的名字叫“铁锈”。 我当时想，‘什么？ 不，C 很棒。” 他的朋友继续说道：“‘不，不，不！ 它让编程再次变得有趣。” 我想，‘不，用 C 语言编程很有趣。’ 他是对的。 我当时就应该这么做。 Rust 实际上很有趣。 它让编程变得有趣。 它让你不必担心编译器，这可以帮助你解决很多问题，并且让代码变得更好一些。”

为什么 Rust 让生活变得如此轻松

因此，Kroah-Hartman 已经从 Rust 怀疑论者转变为内核中最强大的支持者之一。 他现在将 Rust 视为 Linux 的永久组成部分，而不是一个实验。 他的例子很简单：Rust 的所有权和类型系统可以消除大多数主导内核常见漏​​洞和暴露 (CVE) 的“愚蠢的小东西”，同时让劳累过度的维护人员的生活变得更轻松。 简而言之，“Rust 让我的生活变得更加轻松”。

克罗哈特曼应该知道。 他负责监督 Linux 内核 CVE 流程，并且作为稳定内核维护者拥有独特的优势。 他表示，在印度，Linux“每天会看到大约 13 个 CVE”，并且十多年来一直以“每小时几乎九个更改”的速度运行。 他认为，这些漏洞中的大多数不是奇异的攻击，而是简单的 C 错误——未经检查的指针、忘记解锁和草率的清理路径：“这就是我们每天要修复 13 次的问题。 像这样的小、琐碎、小虫子一直都是这样。”

另外：“我不再是程序员了”：Linus Torvalds 谈论他现在使用的唯一两个工具

Rust 如何帮助解决这个问题？ 简单的。 克罗哈特曼继续说道：“这是我的声明，完全不科学。 我见过内核在过去 25 年中所做的每一个 CVE。 我认为 80% 的人会消失，只是因为他们会被 Rust 抓住。” 剩下的 20% 是他更愿意关注的逻辑错误：“Rust 仍然会崩溃。 你可以在 Rust 中做一些非常愚蠢的事情，但所有微小的、愚蠢的、琐碎的事情 Rust 都会为我们处理。”

Kroah-Hartman 始终根据审阅者的工作量而不是开发人员的便利性来选择语言。 Linux 拥有“超过 5,000 名开发人员”，但“约有 150 名核心维护人员负责审查大部分代码”，这种偏差推动了他的优先事项。 “我们针对审稿人进行优化。 我们不会针对开发人员进行优化，因为我们有很多开发人员。”他表示，Rust 在构建时强制执行锁定和生命周期规则的能力意味着审阅者可以将有限的带宽花在逻辑上，而不是簿记上：“如果它作为审阅者构建，我知道这是可以的。 我可以看看逻辑。”

另外：开源安全性一团糟 - IBM 和 Red Hat 押注 50 亿美元，20,000 名工程师可以修复它

Rust 的努力已经在内核中重塑了 C。 为了确保 Rust 绑定的安全，维护人员重新审视了长期存在的 C API，添加了反映 Rust 自动清理功能的防护和作用域分配机制。 “我们有 C 的守卫，范围引用计数，因为 Rust 向我们展示了我们可以做到的，”他说。

“我们向内核和其他 API 添加了更多内存安全内容，因为我们意识到 Rust 向我们展示了我们可以做到这一点，并且我们使 C 代码更可靠、更好。 因此，如果 Rust 明天就会消失，那么内核就会变得更好。”

关键子系统中新工作的默认设置

这种转变已经发生了一段时间。 正如 Kroah-Hartman 于 2025 年在 Linux 内核邮件列表 (LKML) 上写道的那样，“Rust 还使我们能够定义内核内 API，使它们在使用时几乎不可能出错。 我们有太多困难/棘手的 API，需要太多的维护者审查来“确保您做对了”，这是我们的 API 多年来发展的结合（您可以以安全的方式使用“struct cdev”有多少种不同的方式？） 以及 C 不允许我们以更容易/更安全使用的方式表达 API。 迫使这些 API 的维护者重新考虑它们是一件好事，因为它促使我​​们为所有人（包括 C 用户）清理它们，从而使 Linux 整体变得更好。”

另外：红帽现在将永远支持您的 RHEL - 只需支付一定的费用

此外，Rust 正在成为关键子系统中新工作的默认设置。 “某些子系统的新驱动程序只会在 Rust 中被接受。 看看图形子系统，它是当今最复杂的驱动程序；这些人只会使用 Rust，”他说。 Binder 是数十亿设备核心的 Android IPC 机制，现在在内核中具有并行的 C 和 Rust 实现。 C 版本“很快就会消失”，而 Rust 版本“将成为未来所有 Android 设备的基石”。

从形式上来说，内核领导层已经将 Rust 视为一流语言。 “这个实验现在不再是一个实验，”克罗哈特曼说。 “内核社区维护者去年聚集在一起...... 我们说 Rust 实验已经结束。 这是真的。 今后我们将接受这件事。”

另外：IBM 和红帽推出 Lightwell 来保护开源代码免受人工智能攻击

现在，这种转变并不意味着 Linux 内核将用 Rust 重写。 正如他今年早些时候在荷兰乌得勒支举行的 Rust Week 会议上所说，“我们不希望重写，因此除非您是该文件的维护者和所有者，否则只需为新内容重写即可。 保留现有的 C 代码，然后让我们继续发展。”

也就是说，在见证了 Linux 从一开始的成长之后，我感觉到 Linux 内核将用 Rust 重写的那一天可能会到来。 这不会很快发生，但我可以看到它发生。 从设计上来说它更安全。 如果今天有人问我他们应该学习什么语言才能成为内核贡献者，我会告诉他们选择 Rust。

另外：我已经测试了许多便携式 Linux 发行版，但 PorteuX 是我保留在 USB 驱动器上的发行版

与此同时，Kroah-Hartman 承认，对于一个沉浸在 C 语言中的社区（包括他自己）来说，“改变是困难的”：“我喜欢 C。 我从事 C 语言已经很多很多很多年了。” 事实上，他从事这项工作的时间“甚至比这里许多人活着的时间还要长。 这很难。” 但他又回到了最初让他爱上 Rust 的主题：“Rust 再次让编码变得有趣。 我发现它让你不必担心我的参考资料在哪里，我在哪里正确地抓住了我的锁，我做了这个东西吗？我做了这个吗？ 我可以专注于逻辑，这就是它的美妙之处。”

Kroah-Hartman 继续说道，“Rust 对维护人员来说更有趣，对用户来说 Linux 更安全，这是最重要的事情。” 他以熟悉的 Linux 妙语结束：“当然，世界统治正在按计划进行。”
